selectedSlice: Preserve Global Admin Audit and Archive / Exports filters through the existing workspace URL-state contract.
selectionReason: Current repo evidence showed a real protected-workspace continuity gap, not speculative polish. Global Admins could already open exact Recent Audit and failed-export follow-up views, but unlike the other major worklists those sections still dropped their current filters on reload, share, and browser history. Reusing the current URL-state pattern kept the slice small, safe, and route-backed without touching auth, data scope, or visibility boundaries.
candidateShortlist: Global Admin Audit and Archive URL state
userFacingImprovement: Global Admins can now reload or share filtered Audit and Archive / Exports views without losing the current protected-activity or package-follow-up filter.
privacyAndPermissionNotes: The slice changes only protected workspace URL-state handling for existing Global Admin sections.
No auth, RBAC, tenant, site, program, mentor-assignment, or student-privacy boundary changed.
No new routes, API params, or visibility expansions were introduced.
Audit remains Global Admin-only, and Archive / Exports still uses the current redacted package-request data.

validation.notes: Focused workspace and archive dashboard-action checks passed before docs/state closeout, the audit regression test was refreshed for the new completed-row count, and the broader validation ladder passed after the closeout files were updated. git diff --check reported only CRLF normalization warnings.

deferred: Filtered Audit cross-school proof still needs a tighter locally provable scenario before another audit-focused pass.
Broader Global Admin route refresh remains larger than one safe hourly slice.
Presentation ready-for-check-out parity proof refresh is lower value than the selected protected-worklist continuity gap.
Student evidence-panel language cleanup remains open after this admin continuity batch.
Site selector follow-through still needs a broader cross-surface contract.
Mentor reassignment and removal workflow still needs mutation and audit design.
Hosted section-level permission proof still needs allowed runtime and audit-event writes.
Viewer email visibility policy still needs a product decision before any UI change.
Admin import approved-path copy refresh remains lower value than restoring real filtered worklists.
nextRecommended: Inspect filtered Audit cross-school proof next only if the current workspace can prove one exact local scenario; otherwise continue with another bounded Global Admin or student-facing slice.
doNotRepeat: Do not remove Audit action/entityType URL restore while the existing /api/admin/audit-events route already supports those exact filters.
Do not remove Archive / Exports adminExportFilter URL restore while the current section already supports exact failed, in-progress, and complete follow-up filters.
Do not widen Global Admin visibility or invent new audit/export routes while deepening the current filtered worklists.
